mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding supply capacity and economic output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between trailing 3-month INDPRO change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 3%, it signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, the average 60-day SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) compared to non-trigger periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate broad-based consumer demand strength that raises corporate revenues and profits, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if the average forward 20-day return under joint increases is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or if the signal is no better than either series alone (incremental AUC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5%), further declines in the unemployment rate represent an overheated labor market that leads to wage-push inflation and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If in periods with UNRATE below 5%, the correlation between monthly UNRATE change and subsequent 60-day SPX return is not negative (or is positive with p < 0.10), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) signal strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which rapidly translates into positive economic momentum and equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the rank IC of HOUST monthly change versus subsequent 5-day SPX return is less than 0.04 over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers inflation concerns that raise discount rates and erode real earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after CPI YoY exceeds 2.5%, the average 20-day SPX return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or the signal produces more than 50% positive instances, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: The joint pattern of rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a three-month window epitomizes late-cycle monetary tightening that historically precedes equity market declines over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over 3 months and UNRATE falling over 3 months
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the average 60-day return after the joint trigger is not negative (p < 0.10) or if the interaction term in a regression of returns on both series is insignificant (t-stat < 1.96).

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI YoY below 2%), strong growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals healthy consumer demand that supports corporate earnings without overheating, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If in periods with CPIAUCSL YoY below 2%, the rank IC of RSAFS monthly change versus 20-day SPX return is below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.
